_note: ORIGINAL content. Topic coverage follows David Eck's Introduction to Programming Using Java. No prose, examples, or exercises were copied; the source guides curriculum order only.
title: Enums
summary: Represent a small fixed set of named choices.
refresher: An enum defines the complete set of values allowed for a type. Write the choices once, compare them by name, and use a switch when behavior depends on the chosen value. Enum constants are objects, so an enum may also have fields and methods.
refresher_code:
enum Status { NEW, ACTIVE, COMPLETE }
Status current = Status.ACTIVE;

blocks.body: Some values come from a short list that should not grow while the program runs. A course status may be planned, active, or complete. Plain strings allow spelling mistakes, but an enum allows only the declared choices.

blocks.caption: The variable can hold one Status constant. Java checks the name during compilation.

blocks.body: Enum constants are written with the enum type and a dot. They can be compared with `==` because each constant is one fixed object.

blocks.caption: Strings can contain an unnoticed spelling difference. The enum version is checked by the compiler and gives the value a clear type.

blocks.title: Changing text into an enum can fail
blocks.body: `Status.valueOf(text)` requires an exact constant name. Input such as `"active"` does not match `ACTIVE` unless you clean or convert it first.

blocks.prompt: Which assignment is valid for this enum?
blocks.why: An enum variable holds one of its declared constants. The constant is reached through the enum type, so Level.MEDIUM is the valid value.

blocks.body: A switch works well with enums because the choices are known. The compiler can help show when a new constant needs another case.

blocks.why: The value is Level.HIGH, so the HIGH case runs and prints the text 3. The other cases are skipped.

blocks.title: Enum checklist
items: Use an enum for a fixed set of choices.
Store the enum value instead of a matching string.
Use switch when each constant needs different behavior.
